message:
fix(missions): confirm and edit AI prompt before Install-via-AI runs (#5913) (#5917)

Previously, clicking any "Install via AI" button (cert-manager, K3s,
KubeFlex, KubeVirt, OVN, multi-tenancy, and all card install CTAs in
CardWrapper) immediately started the mission. Users had no chance to
see, review, or edit the prompt that was sent to the AI agent.

This change introduces a reusable ConfirmMissionPromptDialog that is
shown after the user clicks an "Install with AI Agent" / "Install for
live data" CTA. The dialog:

- Displays the full AI prompt in an editable TextArea
- Lets the user edit it (add constraints, remove steps, tweak options)
- Has explicit Cancel and "Run mission" buttons
- Blocks Confirm if the prompt is empty

Wired into:
- CardWrapper.tsx (after the cluster-selection step for every card
  install CTA)
- K3sStatus, KubeFlexStatus, KubevirtStatus, OvnStatus
- TenantIsolationSetup (both "Configure All" and per-component install)

i18n keys added under common.json -> confirmMissionPrompt.

Fixes #5913

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,134 @@ +/** + * ConfirmMissionPromptDialog + * + * Shows the AI prompt that will be sent to an "Install via AI" mission + * and lets the user review and edit it before the mission is executed. + * + * Fixes #5913 — Install-via-AI buttons previously started a mission + * immediately with no chance for the user to see or edit the prompt. + * + * Usage: + * setOpen(false)} + * onConfirm={(editedPrompt) => startMission({ ..., initialPrompt: editedPrompt })} + * /> + */ + +import { useState } from 'react' +import { Wand2, Info } from 'lucide-react' +import { useTranslation } from 'react-i18next' +import { BaseModal } from '../../lib/modals/BaseModal' +import { Button } from '../ui/Button' +import { TextArea } from '../ui/TextArea' + +/** Minimum visible rows for the prompt textarea. */ +const PROMPT_TEXTAREA_ROWS = 12 + +interface ConfirmMissionPromptDialogProps { + /** Whether the dialog is open. */ + open: boolean + /** Title of the mission — shown in the modal header. */ + missionTitle: string + /** Short description of the mission — shown under the header title. */ + missionDescription?: string + /** The prompt that will be sent to the AI agent. The user can edit this. */ + initialPrompt: string + /** Called when the user clicks Cancel or dismisses the dialog. */ + onCancel: () => void + /** Called with the (possibly edited) prompt when the user clicks Confirm. */ + onConfirm: (editedPrompt: string) => void +} + +export function ConfirmMissionPromptDialog({ + open, + missionTitle, + missionDescription, + initialPrompt, + onCancel, + onConfirm, +}: ConfirmMissionPromptDialogProps) { + const { t } = useTranslation(['common']) + // Parents of this dialog always conditionally mount it (e.g. + // `{pending && }`) so a new instance + // is created for each pending mission. That means a lazy `useState` + // initializer is enough — we never need to sync with changing props. + const [prompt, setPrompt] = useState(() => initialPrompt) + + const trimmed = prompt.trim() + const confirmDisabled = trimmed.length === 0 + + return ( + + + + +
+ {missionDescription && ( +

{missionDescription}

+ )} + +
+ +

+ {t( + 'confirmMissionPrompt.helpText', + 'Review the prompt below before running. You can edit it to add extra context, change parameters, or remove anything you do not want the AI agent to do.' + )} +

+
